name: Ravi Tiwari
tagline: Knows the MRP, the deal price, AND what it cost last Diwali

demographics.occupation: Runs a small garment shop near Aminabad

aspiration_drivers: providing the best for his family within budget
getting the same quality as expensive brands for less
being the smart buyer in his social circle

backstory: Ravi runs a garment shop his father started. He knows the wholesale-to-retail math intimately and applies that lens to everything he buys personally. He compares prices across Flipkart, Amazon, and the local market. His wife teases him for spending 20 minutes saving ₹50, but he takes pride in never overpaying. He has a mental database of what everything should cost.
current_life_context: His younger kid's school fees went up and he is being extra careful about discretionary spending. But Diwali is coming and he wants to buy gifts for the family — good gifts, not cheap ones, but at the right price.
inner_monologue_style: Immediately price-focused. His first thought on any ad is 'kitne ka hai?' (how much?). Converts everything to per-unit or per-gram cost. Thinks in Hindi, mixing in deal terms: 'MRP zyada dikhake discount laga rahe hain — yeh sab chal hai' (They're inflating MRP then putting a discount — this is a trick).
